title: Windows Rare Parent-Child Process Relationships
description: Identifies unusual parent-child process pairs that deviate from normal system behavior. Rare combinations may indicate process injection, living-off-the-land, or malware execution.
query: ('Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Events' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs') and 'Process Name' != null and 'Parent Process Name' != null | stats count as occurrence, distinctcount('Host Name') as host_count by 'Parent Process Name', 'Process Name' | where occurrence <= 3 and host_count <= 2 | sort occurrence
notes: Normal Windows operations have well-known parent-child relationships (explorer.exe -> notepad.exe). Unusual pairings like winword.exe -> powershell.exe deserve investigation.
